executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with 101 organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate ransomware groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware gang alone compromised 19 Canadian organizations, while the Clop gang targeted 14 entities including law firms and technology providers. Critical infrastructure sectors affected include healthcare, education, public sector services, and financial services—this represents a coordinated mass-exploitation event requiring immediate defensive action.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a devastating ransomware wave affecting 101 organizations across all sectors in 48 hours, including critical healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart College, Collge Superieur De Montreal), public sector (Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, Workers Health & Safety Centre), and critical infrastructure. No CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period despite the severity of the incident wave.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (consumer services/retail) was compromised by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross (automotive retail/consumer services) and Kids & Company (childcare services) were also hit.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers (financial services) was compromised by Sinobi. Multiple accounting firms were hit: Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant. SMT Lawyers (legal/financial services) was targeted by Clop.

report_2w.executive_summary: Critical vulnerabilities are being actively exploited in the wild: Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616, CVSS 9.1) has been added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, and Bomgar RMM (CVE-2026-1731) is seeing a significant uptick in attacks since early April 2026. Iranian state-sponsored group Seedworm is using Microsoft Teams to impersonate IT helpdesk staff and breach organizations globally. ShinyHunters has claimed a major breach of Vercel's infrastructure via a compromised AI tool, exposing source code, API keys, and employee data. Canadian organizations face heightened risk: Canada Life Assurance Company has been listed as a victim by ShinyHunters threat actor group.

items.name: Seedworm (Iranian APT, IRGC-affiliated)
items.targets: Global organizations via Microsoft Teams social engineering
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1534 (Internal Spearphishing), T1586.002 (Compromise Accounts: Email Accounts) - Impersonating IT helpdesk staff via Microsoft Teams following escalating Middle East tensions in February 2026

items.name: Scattered Spider (aka Tyler Buchanan)
items.targets: US organizations in multi-year hacking scheme involving at least 8 million USD theft
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1078 (Valid Accounts) - British hacker Tyler Robert Buchanan pleaded guilty to multi-year US hacking scheme

items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Vercel (infrastructure breach via AI tool OAuth compromise), Canada Life Assurance Company, Booking.com
items.ttps: T1199 (Trusted Relationship), T1528 (Steal Application Access Token), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories) - Compromised third-party AI tool's GitHub OAuth to access Vercel source code, API keys, NPM tokens, and employee data

items.name: UNC1945
items.targets: Managed service providers to target organizations downstream
items.ttps: T1078 (Valid Accounts), T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1584 (Compromise Infrastructure) - Bring Your Own Vulnerable Driver (BYOVD) attacks, exploiting CVE-2020-14871

items.name: FakeWallet Campaign
items.targets: iOS cryptocurrency users via malicious apps in Apple App Store
items.ttps: T1444 (Masquerade as Legitimate Application), T1456 (User Execution), T1533 (Data from Local System) - Phishing apps distributed through official App Store to steal cryptocurrency credentials

items.name: Akira (Ransomware)
items.targets: Integra Architecture, various organizations in retail sector
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - Ransomware dominance in March 2026 threat landscape

items.name: Qilin (Ransomware)
items.targets: Cooperativa De Hospitales De Antioquia - Cohan, Roger D. Mason II P.A., various enterprises
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1071 (Application Layer Protocol) - Ransomware dominance in March 2026 threat landscape

items.name: DragonForce (Ransomware)
items.targets: Multiple organizations across retail and other sectors
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery) - Ransomware activity in March 2026

items.name: Everest (Ransomware)
items.targets: Nutrabio
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service) - Active ransomware attacks in April 2026

items.name: Malicious Browser Extension Campaign
items.targets: 130,000+ users via fake TikTok downloader extensions
items.ttps: T1176 (Browser Extensions), T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie), T1056.004 (Credential API Hooking) - Coordinated campaign targeting browser users with malicious extensions

items.name: n8n Webhook Phishing Operators
items.targets: Various organizations through weaponized n8n automation webhooks
items.ttps: T1566.002 (Phishing: Spearphishing Link), T1608.005 (Stage Capabilities: Link Target) - Phishing campaigns since October 2025 leveraging legitimate n8n webhooks to distribute malware

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian organizations face direct threat from ShinyHunters breach disclosure affecting Canada Life Assurance Company, exposed in data leak campaign. No official CCCS advisories were identified in this reporting period. Economic and geopolitical tensions with the United States are dominating Canadian news, with Prime Minister Mark Carney stating Canada's strong economic ties to the US are now a 'weakness to be corrected.'
canada_landscape.retailers: None identified in breach reports, though retail sector mentioned as target in general March 2026 ransomware landscape analysis.
canada_landscape.financial: Canada Life Assurance Company (canadalife.com) reported as victim by ShinyHunters threat actor group in data breach.
